title: k8s-watcher: verify jobs posted without spec field fail immediately
description: Watcher posts verify tasks to daemon inbox without `spec` field; inbox buildPrompts throws 'verify task requires `spec` (path to spec file)' and job fails. Observed 2026-06-10 renzo cluster: 3 failed watcher-posted verify jobs for renzo-agent rollouts. Fix: watcherConfigFromEnv reads SPECIFY_SPEC_INLINE_PATH into specPath; triggerVerifyForRollout includes spec in payload; startK8sWatcher skips posting (with log) when no spec configured. Files: src/agent/k8s-watcher.ts, src/agent/k8s-watcher.test.ts
close_reason: Fixed in PR #45 (merged): watcher forwards SPECIFY_SPEC_INLINE_PATH as spec in verify payloads, skips posting when unset

title: daemon: persist inbox job state, mark in-flight jobs interrupted on restart
description: Inbox jobs held in memory only (InboxRegistry.history Map, src/daemon/inbox.ts). Pod restart mid-run (observed 2026-06-10, verify job msg_8496dc2f lost to deployment rollout) loses the job; pollers get 404/000 forever, no terminal status. Fix: file-per-job JSON registry under .specify/inbox/_registry (on /work PVC), persist on submit + status change, restoreFromDisk() on daemon boot marks queued/running as status=interrupted with error reason, GET /inbox/:id serves restored records. Add inbox:interrupted as terminal SSE event.

title: EPIC: Learning specify - in-house memory + cooperative QA + skill auto-creation
description: Build specify into an agent that learns from cooperative QA sessions. Built directly on the existing Claude Agent SDK runtime in src/. Four phases: (1) in-house memory mechanisms - FTS5 session search, three-layer persistence, optional Honcho dialectic provider via MCP, prompt evolution loop; (2) cooperative QA Tier 1 - webapp feedback panel + ingest pipeline; (3) cooperative QA Tier 2 - Playwright CDP-attached browser sharing user context; (4) skill auto-creation - pattern miner + draft synthesizer + review UI. Direction approved 2026-04-27 after research showed external wrapper-runtime paths cannot use Max subscription billing.
close_reason: All 16 children shipped. Specify now has: MemoryProvider abstraction (SP-y0y), SQLite+FTS5 session indexer (SP-j1d), three-layer prompt context (SP-k9w), optional Honcho dialectic provider (SP-0l2), prompt evolution loop (SP-0xk), cooperative-QA Tier 1 - webapp feedback panel + ingest endpoint (SP-2d5/SP-a7a), in-session sibling propagation (SP-9ei), confidence model (SP-3yl), Playwright CDP-attach for shared browser context (SP-olt), session replay store (SP-390), context-rich feedback (SP-d9c), pattern miner (SP-l22), skill draft synthesizer (SP-a71), drafts review pane + approve/reject (SP-bgd), approved-skill replay (SP-5i3). 108 tests green; webapp bundles cleanly.

title: Pick hermes orchestrator inference path (cheap thinker)
description: Hermes' own loop cannot use Anthropic OAuth (routes to empty extra_usage pool per issues #6475, #12905). Needs alternative. Options: Nous Portal free tier (designed for hermes), local Ollama/llama.cpp, OpenRouter free routes. Decision criteria: zero ongoing cost, latency tolerable for orchestration, JSON tool calling reliable.
close_reason: Superseded: pivoting to in-house implementation on top of existing Claude Agent SDK runtime. External wrapper-runtime path blocked by Anthropic third-party-OAuth ban (effective 2026-01-09), so subscription billing flows only through user-invoked claude CLI. New plan re-implements memory + skill-creation mechanisms directly in specify's TS code.

title: Verify claude-code skill routes through Max subscription (no API spend)
description: Hermes claude-code skill delegates via 'claude -p' subprocess. Claude Code uses Max OAuth. End-to-end test: invoke claude-code skill from hermes, confirm zero API charges + work completes. Without this, whole architecture defaults to API billing - deal-breaker per user (gmecocci, Max x20).
close_reason: Superseded: pivoting to in-house implementation on top of existing Claude Agent SDK runtime. External wrapper-runtime path blocked by Anthropic third-party-OAuth ban (effective 2026-01-09), so subscription billing flows only through user-invoked claude CLI. New plan re-implements memory + skill-creation mechanisms directly in specify's TS code.

title: Install hermes-agent + Python 3.11 toolchain
description: System python is 3.9; hermes needs 3.11+. uv already installed. Run hermes installer per https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent. Verify ~/.hermes structure. Confirm CLI works standalone before integration.
close_reason: hermes-agent v0.11.0 cloned to ~/.hermes/hermes-agent, uv venv with Python 3.11.15, core deps installed, hermes CLI verified

title: EPIC: Rebuild specify on hermes-agent (logic in hermes, TS = UI shell)
description: Migrate all agent logic to NousResearch/hermes-agent. TS specify becomes thin client: webapp + CLI wrapper + spec.yaml editor only. All learning, agent loop, memory, skills move to hermes via skill pack. Direction approved 2026-04-27.
close_reason: Superseded: pivoting to in-house implementation on top of existing Claude Agent SDK runtime. New direction supersedes the external wrapper-runtime path.

title: [qa-cli-setup] specify: implement qa-cli You are implementing a qa-cli binary for repo ...
description:
[qa-cli-setup] specify: implement qa-cli You are implementing a qa-cli binary for repo `specify` at `/Users/gmecocci/projects/specify`. This is a cross-repo task: the target repo is NOT the Argos repo. You are working inside the target repo's worktree. ## Goal Argos has detected that this repo uses Specify for QA sessions but has no `qa-cli` binary. Your job: implement one. ## Contract (verbatim from Argos's docs/qa-cli-contract.md) # qa-cli Contract This document defines the interface between Argos and any repository that integrates QA run replay. Each repository ships its own `qa-cli` binary (or symlink) that implements this contract. Argos shells out to the binary and parses JSON — it does **not** speak a daemon protocol directly. --- ## See also - [`specify-cli` contract](specify-cli-contract.md) — spec verification / flagged-item contract. - [`external-feedback-cli` contract](external-feedback-cli-contract.md) — external feedback ingest contract. ### Subset compatibility and symlink dispatch A single repo-side binary can satisfy both the `specify-cli` contract and the `qa-cli` contract simultaneously. Two supported patterns: 1. **Symlinks** — ship `specify-cli` as the real binary, create `qa-cli` → `specify-cli` (or vice versa). Implement all subcommands from both contracts. Since both contracts define `status` with the same `SpecifyStatus` shape, there is no conflict. 2. **`argv[0]` dispatch** — detect `path.basename(process.argv[1])` at startup and branch between the two command sets. Useful when both CLIs share significant implementation. Argos maintains **separate discovery chains** for each CLI — no auto-fallback occurs between them. --- ## Discovery order Argos resolves `qa-cli` via the following chain (first match wins): 1. `qa.qaCli` path from the repo config (explicit override) 2. `<repo>/.bin/qa-cli` 3. `<repo>/node_modules/.bin/qa-cli` 4. `which qa-cli` (PATH search) If none resolves, QA replay integration is silently disabled for that repo. Auth is entirely delegated to the CLI. The CLI reads its own credentials (keychain, env vars, config files). Argos does not pass tokens. --- ## Exit codes | Code | Meaning | |------|--------------------------| | 0 | Success | | 1 | Usage / bad arguments | | 2 | Auth failure | | 3 | Daemon unreachable | | 4 | Run / resource not found | Any non-zero exit that does not match a code above is treated as a generic error by Argos. --- ## TypeScript schemas ```ts type QaArtifactKind = "trace" | "video" | "har" | "console" | "screenshot"; interface QaRunSummary { id: string; startedAt: string; // ISO 8601 completedAt?: string; // ISO 8601; absent if still running spec: string; // spec file path or suite name pass: boolean; durationMs: number; trigger?: string; // e.g. "bead", "manual", "schedule" beadId?: string; // bead that triggered this run, if any } interface QaArtifact { kind: QaArtifactKind; path?: string; // local absolute path (if available) url?: string; // remote URL (if available) sizeBytes?: number; } interface QaRunDetail extends QaRunSummary { artifacts: QaArtifact[]; stdoutTail?: string; // last N lines of stdout errorMessage?: string; // human-readable error if pass === false } interface QaRerunResult { taskId: string; queuedAt: string; // ISO 8601 } ``` --- ## Subcommands ### `qa-cli status [--repo=<path>] [--json]` Report whether the QA daemon is reachable. **Flags** | Flag | Type | Description | |------|------|-------------| | `--repo` | string | Path to the repository root. Defaults to `cwd`. | | `--json` | flag | Emit JSON output (always emitted; flag accepted for consistency). | **Stdout** — JSON object (one line). Uses the same `SpecifyStatus` shape for parity — both CLIs share the same status response format, enabling shared rendering in the Argos dashboard. ```json { "running": true, "mode": "local", "endpoint": "http://127.0.0.1:4200", "specPath": "/path/to/repo/playwright.config.ts", "lastVerifyAt": "2026-05-26T08:00:00Z" } ``` | Field | Type | Required | Description | |-------|------|----------|-------------| | `running` | boolean | yes | Whether the QA daemon / CLI backend is operational. | | `mode` | `"local" \| "remote"` | yes | `"local"` = on-machine; `"remote"` = cloud / off-machine. | | `endpoint` | string | no | Daemon URL if HTTP-accessible. | | `specPath` | string | yes | Absolute path to the active spec / config file. | | `lastVerifyAt` | ISO 8601 string | no | Timestamp of the most recent successful QA run. | --- ### `qa-cli runs list [--limit=N] [--cursor=...] [--json]` Return paginated QA run summaries, newest first. **Flags** | Flag | Type | Default | Description | |------|------|---------|-------------| | `--limit` | integer | 20 | Maximum number of runs to return. | | `--cursor` | string (opaque) | — | Resume pagination from this cursor. | | `--json` | flag | — | Emit JSON output. | **Stdout** — JSON object (one line): ```json { "runs": [ { "id": "run-abc123", "startedAt": "2026-05-26T08:00:00Z", "completedAt": "2026-05-26T08:02:30Z", "spec": "tests/login.spec.ts", "pass": true, "durationMs": 150000, "trigger": "bead", "beadId": "argos-xyz" } ], "cursor": "eyJwYWdlIjoyfQ==" } ``` | Field | Type | Required | Description | |-------|------|----------|-------------| | `runs` | array | yes | Run summaries. Empty array if none. | | `runs[].id` | string | yes | Stable unique run identifier. | | `runs[].startedAt` | ISO 8601 | yes | When the run started. | | `runs[].completedAt` | ISO 8601 | no | When the run finished. Absent if still running. | | `runs[].spec` | string | yes | Spec file path or suite name. | | `runs[].pass` | boolean | yes | Whether the run passed. | | `runs[].durationMs` | number | yes | Total duration in milliseconds. | | `runs[].trigger` | string | no | What triggered the run. | | `runs[].beadId` | string | no | Associated bead ID. | | `cursor` | string | yes | Opaque pagination cursor. Empty string means no more pages. | --- ### `qa-cli runs get <id> --json` Return full detail for a single run including artifacts. **Arguments** | Arg | Description | |-----|-------------| | `<id>` | Run ID (from `runs list`). | **Flags** | Flag | Description | |------|-------------| | `--json` | Emit JSON output. | **Stdout** — JSON object (one line): ```json { "id": "run-abc123", "startedAt": "2026-05-26T08:00:00Z", "completedAt": "2026-05-26T08:02:30Z", "spec": "tests/login.spec.ts", "pass": true, "durationMs": 150000, "artifacts": [ { "kind": "trace", "path": "/tmp/qa-runs/run-abc123/trace.zip", "sizeBytes": 204800 }, { "kind": "video", "url": "https://storage.example.com/qa/run-abc123/video.mp4", "sizeBytes": 1048576 } ], "stdoutTail": " 2 passed (2.5s)\n" } ``` Exits `4` if the run ID is not found. --- ### `qa-cli runs artifact <id> <kind> [--format=stream|path]` Retrieve a specific artifact for a run. **Arguments** | Arg | Description | |-----|-------------| | `<id>` | Run ID. | | `<kind>` | Artifact kind: `trace \| video \| har \| console \| screenshot`. | **Flags** | Flag | Default | Description | |------|---------|-------------| | `--format` | `stream` | `stream` — write bytes to stdout; `path` — print local absolute path to stdout. | In `stream` mode, stdout contains raw binary bytes (e.g. a zip file). Argos pipes these directly to the HTTP response or writes them to disk. In `path` mode, stdout contains a single newline-terminated absolute filesystem path. The artifact must already exist locally; Argos will not download it. Exits `4` if the run or artifact is not found. --- ### `qa-cli runs rerun <id>` Queue a re-run of an existing run. Non-blocking — the caller polls `runs get <taskId>` for the result. **Arguments** | Arg | Description | |-----|-------------| | `<id>` | Run ID to re-run. | **Stdout** — JSON object (one line): ```json { "taskId": "task-xyz789", "queuedAt": "2026-05-26T09:00:00Z" } ``` | Field | Type | Required | Description | |-------|------|----------|-------------| | `taskId` | string | yes | ID of the newly queued task (becomes a run ID once started). | | `queuedAt` | ISO 8601 | yes | When the task was enqueued. | Exits `4` if the source run ID is not found. --- ## Reference implementation The file `scripts/mock-qa-cli` in this repository is an executable script that implements every subcommand above with hardcoded fixture responses. Use it: - **Manual testing** — `scripts/mock-qa-cli status` - **Smoke-test harness** — the `scripts/test-qa-cli.sh` script sets `cliPath` to this mock and exercises the Argos invoker without a real daemon. - **Reference for integrators** — shows the expected output format precisely. ```bash # Quick sanity check scripts/mock-qa-cli status scripts/mock-qa-cli runs list --limit=2 scripts/mock-qa-cli runs get run-pass-001 --json scripts/mock-qa-cli runs artifact run-pass-001 trace --format=path scripts/mock-qa-cli runs rerun run-pass-001 ``` --- ## Integrator checklist To add `qa-cli` support to a repository: 1. Ship a `qa-cli` binary (or wrapper script) in `<repo>/.bin/` or on `PATH`. 2. Implement all subcommands and exit codes described above. 3. Handle authentication internally (keychain, env, config file — your choice). 4. Emit JSON on stdout; send human-readable errors to stderr. 5. Ensure exit codes match the table above. 6. Optionally use symlinks or `argv[0]` dispatch to share implementation with `specify-cli` (see Subset compatibility above). --- ## Version history | Date | Change | |------|--------| | 2026-05-26 | v1 — initial contract (argos-7nji) | ## Approach 1. Inspect this repo: how does it run Specify? Look for `.specify/`, `qa/`, daemon config, or related scripts. 2. Implement a binary at `.bin/qa-cli` (executable; shebang matching the project's primary stack). 3. CLI must implement all subcommands per the contract: `status`, `runs list`, `runs get <id>`, `runs artifact <id> <kind>`, `runs rerun <id>`. Match the contract's JSON output schema exactly. 4. Configure auth/endpoint internals via env vars or a per-repo config file — DO NOT require any token from Argos. 5. Test the CLI manually or write a minimal smoke test. 6. Open a PR in THIS repo (NOT Argos) titled "Add qa-cli for Argos QA integration". 7. Reply when the PR is open with: PR URL + 2-3 sentence summary of how the CLI connects to Specify + any caveats. ## Out of scope - Do NOT modify Argos config files. - Do NOT touch the Argos repo at all. If the repo's QA setup is unclear, STOP and report what you found instead of guessing.

Acceptance criteria:
- The reported request is implemented or explicitly explained as blocked.
- Relevant tests or deploy checks are recorded in the completion evidence.
notes:
Argos needs user input:

Can you enable a publish path so I can push this qa-cli change and open the PR?

title: Install Java runtime for Sonar scanner
description: Install a local Java runtime on the development machine so npm run sonar can execute sonar-scanner, then verify the scanner starts successfully.
close_reason: Installed Homebrew OpenJDK 21, linked it into Homebrew and user JavaVirtualMachines discovery, and verified npm run sonar -- --version succeeds.

title: Set up strict linting and static analysis
description: Add strict project linting, formatting, and local static analysis configuration for the TypeScript CLI/server, React webapp, and infrastructure files. Include runnable npm scripts and document how to use them.
close_reason: Added strict ESLint quality gate, Prettier/Sonar config, webapp typecheck, lint cleanup, and verified quality/tests/audit.

title: API surface reduction — phase 1 (mechanical cuts)
description:
Reduce CLI + daemon API surface. Phase 1 = mechanical cuts with no behavior changes. See follow-up phase-2 issue for opinionated cuts that need usage evidence first.

# Cuts

1. **Drop `POST /verify` and `POST /capture` daemon convenience routes.** Pure wrappers around `POST /inbox` with task field. Update README + describe manifest if referenced.
2. **Drop top-level `specify lint` alias.** Keep canonical `specify spec lint` only. Update help text + commands manifest.
3. **Collapse `specify serve` + `specify ui` into `specify review`.** Three commands launch the same webapp. Keep one: `specify review`. Add `--background` flag for daemonize (currently `ui start`) and `--stop` flag for kill (currently `ui stop`). Delete `serve.ts` and `ui.ts` command files. Update help + commands manifest. Document migration in commit message.
4. **Collapse `specify human` sub-modes to one canonical chat REPL.** Drop the `repl`, `shell`, `watch`, `tui`, `wizard`, `init` sub-verbs. `specify human` always launches the chat REPL. Delete src/cli/interactive/{repl.ts, tui.ts, wizard.ts}. Keep src/cli/interactive/chat.ts.
5. **Delete `src/agent/prompt-evolution.ts` + its test.** Zero non-test imports. Confirmed dead code by grep.

# Acceptance

- Each cut is a separate commit in the same PR for clean revertability.
- All 210 existing tests pass after the cuts.
- TypeScript build green.
- Help text + commands-manifest updated.
- README updated where it documented removed routes/commands.
- PR titled "SP-pass1: API surface reduction phase 1".

# Why

Smaller surface = less to document, less to test, less to maintain, less for installer agents to navigate. None of these have load-bearing usage we know of.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #24 — net -1796 LOC. /verify+/capture routes, top-level lint alias, serve+ui commands, human sub-modes, prompt-evolution module all gone. 205/205 pass.

title: Pending-decisions queue with scoped approval (narrow/medium/broad)
description:
Add pending-decisions inbox so the verify agent can pause on ambiguity and surface a typed question to a human, who resolves it at one of three scopes — and the resolution hot-loads into memory.

Pattern lifted from Temper's authz convergence flow (see https://github.com/nerdsane/temper). Today the agent has two outbound options: file a ticket (file_ticket) or write a memory row (memory_record). It has no third option: "I'm not sure if this is a bug or intentional — pause and ask." That gap means runs either fail-closed (file a noisy ticket the human dismisses) or fail-open (silently log to memory and keep going). A pending-decisions queue gives the agent a third channel and gives the human a typed UI: pick one of N pre-drafted resolutions at narrow / medium / broad scope.

Scope semantics:
- narrow: this run only — overrides agent behavior for the rest of the run, no persistence
- medium: this behavior_id within this spec — persists as a memory row scoped to (specId, behavior_id)
- broad: this spec — persists as a memory row scoped to (specId)

Acceptance:
- New MCP tool `mcp__decisions__file_decision` exposed inside verify task. Args: question, context, proposed_resolutions[] each with {scope, label, action_hint}, blocking (bool — pause run vs continue).
- Daemon HTTP: GET /decisions (list open), POST /decisions/:id/resolve {scope, resolution_index}, GET /decisions/:id (poll for resolution).
- Persistence: decisions store on disk (JSONL append, similar to runs).
- Resolved decisions at medium/broad scope write a memory row via the existing memory layer.
- Cooperative-QA webapp gains a "Decisions" tab listing open queue + 1-click resolve.
- Surfaced in `specify deploy describe` agent_tools alongside file_ticket.
- Tests: file_decision via MCP handler; resolve endpoint; medium-scope writes memory; webapp inbox lists open decisions.

Why this issue exists: closes the missing inbound channel between agent and human during verify, mirrors Temper's policy convergence pattern, and feeds memory rows from real ambiguity instead of the agent's guesses.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #22 — pending-decisions MCP tool, daemon endpoints, webapp tab. 196 tests pass.

title: specify-qa: feedback-mcp tool for agent-filed tickets
description: Give the verify agent an MCP tool (mcp__feedback__file_ticket) that files outbound tickets to bd or to a configurable HTTP sink (SPECIFY_FEEDBACK_URL). Distinct from the existing src/agent/feedback.ts which ingests inbound flags from the webapp.
close_reason: Merged in PR #19

title: specify-qa: enrich 'deploy describe' with target_contract + trigger_models
description: Add target_contract block (expected_scheme, response timeout, egress, tls) + trigger_models block (watch/webhook/both/none with model + tradeoff text + 'cron' marked not_implemented) to the describe manifest. Documents semantics installer agents need to make correct choices.
close_reason: Merged in PR #18

title: specify-qa: wire SPECIFY_TARGET_URL as verify default
description: TF module sets SPECIFY_TARGET_URL but no code reads it. Wire daemon/inbox to use it as the default url when an inbox message has no explicit url field. Spec's target.url remains the fallback when env is unset.
close_reason: Merged in PR #18

title: specify-qa: Terraform module deploy/terraform/modules/specify-qa
description: Terraform module with main.tf (Deployment+Service+PVC+ConfigMap+Secret), variables.tf (mutually-exclusive target_url/target_dns/target_cluster_ip/target_from_configmap, mutually-exclusive spec_inline/spec_url/spec_git, discovery block, report_sinks list), outputs.tf (inbox_url, inbox_bearer, service_dns), rbac.tf (opt-in Role/RoleBinding for k8s watch). README in module dir.
close_reason: Merged in PR #15

title: specify-qa: k8s-watcher.ts
description: src/agent/k8s-watcher.ts using @kubernetes/client-node informer. Watch Deployments + StatefulSets across configured namespaces, label-filtered (default selector specify.dev/target=true). On rollout-complete (status.observedGeneration == status.updatedReplicas), POST to local daemon /inbox with {kind:'verify_target', target, ref}. Opt-in via env SPECIFY_K8S_WATCH=true; namespace list via SPECIFY_K8S_NAMESPACES.
close_reason: Merged in PR #14

title: specify-qa: 'specify deploy describe' + 'print-tf' commands
description: Self-describing install commands. 'specify deploy describe --format=json' emits structured JSON: image digest, TF module source+ref, required_inputs, oneof_groups (target/spec/discovery/report_sinks options), secrets_to_create, outputs, examples, agent_install_recipe. 'specify deploy print-tf <preset>' emits a working .tf file for preset (minimal|watch-mode|webhook-mode|gitops-spec). Goal: another agent in another repo can read describe output and generate a correct specify-qa.tf without human.
close_reason: Merged in PR #16

title: specify-qa: Dockerfile + GHCR publish workflow
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile producing ghcr.io/gm2211/specify-qa:<tag>. Stages: tsc build, vite webapp build, playwright chromium install. Final image runs 'specify daemon'. Add .github/workflows/publish-image.yml: on tag push, build + push to GHCR. Image must be public (free tier on GHCR + zero-touch installs).
notes: PR #11 opened. Multi-stage Dockerfile bundles webapp via vite, default CMD = daemon, HOME=/work for PVC-friendly state, tini PID 1, GHCR workflow renames image to specify-qa.
close_reason: Merged in PR #11

title: specify-qa: report-sink.ts (file + slack)
description: src/agent/report-sink.ts — pluggable outbound publisher. Sinks: file (writes to /work/reports/<run_id>.json), slack (POST webhook). Config via /work/config/sinks.yaml mounted from ConfigMap. Hook into verify run completion. github_check sink deferred to later issue.
close_reason: Merged in PR #13

title: specify-qa: spec-loader.ts (inline/url/git)
description: src/agent/spec-loader.ts — three loaders selected by env: SPECIFY_SPEC_INLINE (path on disk from ConfigMap mount), SPECIFY_SPEC_URL (+ optional bearer from SPECIFY_SPEC_BEARER_FILE), SPECIFY_SPEC_GIT (repo+ref+path, deploy key from file). Returns parsed spec. Re-fetch on POST /control/reload-spec. Tag memory rows with spec_hash on version change.
close_reason: Merged in PR #12

title: EPIC: specify-qa k8s deploy package
description: Ship specify as a containerized QA agent runnable in any Kubernetes cluster via Terraform module. Per-spec pod, PVC-backed memory, flexible target/spec wiring, optional cluster-watch discovery. Consumer experience: 'agent installs specify-qa into existing TF repo with zero hand-holding'.
notes: Designed in chat. 8 sub-issues track concrete deliverables.
close_reason: All sub-issues closed; epic complete

title: Webapp feedback panel: free-text + per-event flag + kind tagging
description: Side panel in webapp activity stream (commit 243967f) where user inline-flags timeline events. Each feedback carries kind in {missed_check, false_positive, important_pattern, ignore_pattern, file_bug}, free-text comment, target event id, session id. UX: hover-flag affordance on each timeline row, plus session-level free-text input.
close_reason: Cooperative-QA feedback channel shipped end-to-end. src/agent/feedback.ts: ingestFeedback() routes by kind (note / important_pattern / missed_check / false_positive / ignore_pattern / file_bug), writes to specify.observations.yaml with provenance, spawns bd CLI for file_bug, emits feedback:ingested + feedback:propagate_pattern events. POST /api/feedback in src/review/server.ts. webapp/src/api.ts: postFeedback(). webapp/src/components/FeedbackForm.tsx + ActivityStream inline flag affordance + session-level feedback. CSS in globals.css. 5 new tests; full suite 65/65; webapp bundles cleanly.

title: Feedback ingest endpoint: writes memory + files beads on actionable kinds
description: Specify daemon receives feedback events, writes to MemoryProvider, files beads automatically when kind=file_bug (with feedback text as description), updates skill confidence stats for kind in {missed_check, false_positive}, queues sibling-check work when kind=important_pattern.
close_reason: Cooperative-QA feedback channel shipped end-to-end. src/agent/feedback.ts: ingestFeedback() routes by kind (note / important_pattern / missed_check / false_positive / ignore_pattern / file_bug), writes to specify.observations.yaml with provenance, spawns bd CLI for file_bug, emits feedback:ingested + feedback:propagate_pattern events. POST /api/feedback in src/review/server.ts. webapp/src/api.ts: postFeedback(). webapp/src/components/FeedbackForm.tsx + ActivityStream inline flag affordance + session-level feedback. CSS in globals.css. 5 new tests; full suite 65/65; webapp bundles cleanly.

title: Three-layer persistence: project / user / per-spec observations
description: Per-project: ./CLAUDE.md (already exists). Per-user: ~/.specify/memory.md (rules with why+how-to-apply). Per-spec: specify.observations.yaml (sibling of specify.spec.yaml, derived behaviors with provenance fields source/session/created_at/confidence). Loader merges all three into agent context at session start.
close_reason: Three-layer prompt context shipped in src/agent/memory-layers.ts. User layer (~/.specify/memory.md), project layer (SPECIFY.md or CLAUDE.md), per-spec observations (specify.observations.yaml v1 schema with id/description/area_id/behavior_id/source/session_id/created_at/confidence). Loader merges all three into prompt preamble with confidence floor + budget-bounded trimming. sdk-runner applies layered context to all tasks. 9 new tests; full suite 60/60.

title: MemoryProvider TS interface; default file-backed implementation
description: Abstract interface in src/memory/: read(query), write(entry), prefetch(session_ctx), shutdown(). Default impl writes to the three-layer files. Allows swap-in alternative providers without touching agent loop.
close_reason: Added MemoryProvider interface in src/agent/memory-provider.ts with FileBackedMemoryProvider default impl. Refactored memory-mcp.ts and sdk-runner.ts to consume the provider abstraction. 4 new tests pass; full suite green (46/46).

title: SQLite+FTS5 session transcript indexer
description: Index every session's user messages, agent replies, tool calls, and outputs into a SQLite FTS5 table for cross-session full-text recall. Schema: (session_id, ts, role, kind, content, tags). Replaces ad-hoc learned-memory store from commit 407edab. Supports MATCH queries from agent context loaders.
close_reason: Added SQLite+FTS5 session transcript indexer in src/agent/session-store.ts. Schema: sessions + events + events_fts virtual table, automatic insert/update/delete triggers keep FTS in sync. SessionStore.attachToEventBus subscribes to the event bus and lazily ensures session rows. sdk-runner now opens the store at run start, attaches it for the lifetime of runSpecifyAgent, detaches+closes in finally. 5 new tests pass; full suite 51/51 green.

title: Zellij sidebar spinner: not working in sandbox, missing question-mark for blocked prompts
description:
## Problem

Two issues with the Zellij sidebar status indicator:

### 1. Spinner not visible in sandbox sessions
When running Claude inside a Docker sandbox (clauded/cll), the spinner never shows in the Zellij sidebar. Works (sometimes) for regular Claude sessions in a Zellij pane but never for sandboxed sessions.

### 2. No question-mark indicator when Claude is waiting for user input
Even in regular (non-sandbox) sessions within Zellij, there's no distinct indicator when Claude is blocked waiting for user input (permission prompt, question). This matters because the user can't tell at a glance that Claude stopped making progress and needs attention.

## Expected Behavior
- Spinner should work in both regular and sandbox Claude sessions
- A distinct indicator (e.g. ?) should appear when Claude is asking a question or waiting for permission

## Context
Status line and notification hooks are configured in functions.zsh and the Docker entrypoint (ensure-plugins.sh). The sandbox sets up notification hooks but spinner state may not propagate through the Docker boundary.
close_reason: deleted on request

title: inbox verify: fall back to env spec source when request omits spec
description: Deeper fix beyond SP-979: buildPrompts in src/daemon/inbox.ts:378 hard-fails when verify request lacks `spec`. Daemon already knows its spec source via specSourceFromEnv (src/agent/spec-loader.ts) — SPECIFY_SPEC_INLINE_PATH / SPECIFY_SPEC_URL / SPECIFY_SPEC_GIT_REPO. Inbox could resolve from env when request omits spec, so ANY sender (webhook, watcher, manual curl) gets working verify without knowing pod-internal mount paths. Note url/git sources need resolveSpec (async fetch) not just loadSpec(path) — small refactor of buildPrompts. SP-979 fixes only watcher payload + inline path.

title: Adopt repository-wide Prettier formatting baseline
description: Prettier config and scripts exist, but the existing repository has not been normalized. Run npm run format:write in a dedicated formatting-only change, review generated diffs, and then make npm run format suitable for inclusion in the default quality gate.

# Acceptance

For each item: one-line verdict KEEP / KILL / GATE with evidence. File individual cut PRs for kills.

title: API surface reduction — phase 2 (audit-gated)
description:
Phase 2 of API surface reduction. Each cut needs usage evidence first — is it actually invoked from automation, scripts, or active user flows? Collect data before committing.

# Audit + decide

- `specify replay` task + CLI command — niche traffic replay. If unused, kill.
- `specify compare` task + CLI command — niche remote-vs-local diff. Same call.
- `specify impersonate` — MockServer spinup. Could be a sibling tool, not a subcommand.
- `specify clean` — `rm -rf .specify/` does the same.
- `freeform` agent task — runner branch, unclear active use.
- `specify create` vs `specify human` chat — both bootstrap a spec. Collapse?
- `GET /sessions` + `POST /sessions/:id/close` daemon routes — audit external usage.
- `memory-layers.ts` vs `memory-provider.ts` — possible double abstraction. Read both, pick one.
- pattern-miner + pattern-propagator + skill-synthesizer chain — wired in sdk-runner but unclear if it's earning weight in real runs. Audit output volume; gate behind feature flag if speculative; remove if not.

# Acceptance

For each item: a one-line verdict — KEEP / KILL / GATE — with evidence. Then file individual cut PRs for the kills.

title: Per-tool call budgets in MCP runner
description:
Add per-tool max-calls-per-run budgets to the SDK runner so a runaway agent can't fire file_ticket / memory_record / file_decision in a loop.

Today every MCP tool we register (memory_record, memory_list, file_ticket, and the upcoming file_decision) has no rate limit. A misbehaving agent on a flaky target can file 200 tickets, write 500 memory rows, or otherwise burn budget. Temper enforces per-call resource budgets on its WASM integrations for the same reason; we should do the structurally simpler version.

Acceptance:
- ToolBudget config map keyed by tool name, with default cap (e.g. file_ticket: 10, memory_record: 50, file_decision: 5 per run).
- Runner tracks per-run counters; when cap exceeded, the tool handler returns a structured error and emits a `tool:budget_exceeded` event.
- Configurable via env (SPECIFY_TOOL_BUDGET_<TOOL>) and via spec-level override.
- Surfaced in `specify deploy describe` per agent_tools entry as `budget_default`.
- Tests: cap enforced after N calls; env override works; budget reset between runs.

Why this issue exists: cheap safety property, prevents runaway tool invocation, matches Temper's resource-budget pattern from temper-wasm.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #23 — per-tool call budgets, 14 new tests, 210/210 pass.

title: Approved skill replay path: autonomous re-execution next session
description: Agent context loader picks up newly-approved skills and lists them as available verifications. Agent invokes them autonomously when relevant (matching target/area), skipping user. Replay records per-skill success rate which feeds confidence model.
close_reason: Drafts review pane (SP-bgd) shipped: GET /api/skill-drafts, POST approve/reject, GET /api/skills/active. webapp/src/components/SkillDraftsPanel.tsx with expand/approve/reject + body preview, polled every 15s. Approved skill replay (SP-5i3) shipped: promoteDraft moves the draft to .specify/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (status flipped to approved), refuses overwrite collisions; listActiveSkills + renderActiveSkillsPrompt produce a prompt preamble injected into every spec-aware run by sdk-runner so approved skills replay autonomously next session. 4 new tests; full suite 98/98; webapp bundles cleanly.

title: Skill draft synthesizer: generate SKILL.md from observed sequence
description: Given a pattern + example sessions, LLM-generate a draft skill file (markdown with frontmatter + workflow steps + tool sequence). Stored in pending drafts dir until user reviews.
close_reason: Skill draft synthesizer in src/agent/skill-synthesizer.ts. synthesizeDraft writes pattern-derived SKILL.md drafts to .specify/skill-drafts/<id>.md with frontmatter (name, description, version, status:pending, metadata.specify with pattern_id/signature/sessionCount/occurrences). Pluggable describer: heuristic default produces a usable starter; callers can pass an LLM-backed describer for polished output. listDrafts + setDraftStatus power the review UI (SP-bgd). Embedded minimal yaml encoder/decoder avoids a runtime dep just for a small frontmatter shape. 5 new tests; full suite 94/94.

title: Drafts review pane in webapp: approve/edit/reject
description: Webapp page listing pending skill drafts with diff vs accepted skills, examples that triggered each, accept/edit/reject actions. Approved drafts move to ~/.specify/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and become replayable. Rejected drafts logged for pattern-miner negative training.
close_reason: Drafts review pane (SP-bgd) shipped: GET /api/skill-drafts, POST approve/reject, GET /api/skills/active. webapp/src/components/SkillDraftsPanel.tsx with expand/approve/reject + body preview, polled every 15s. Approved skill replay (SP-5i3) shipped: promoteDraft moves the draft to .specify/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (status flipped to approved), refuses overwrite collisions; listActiveSkills + renderActiveSkillsPrompt produce a prompt preamble injected into every spec-aware run by sdk-runner so approved skills replay autonomously next session. 4 new tests; full suite 98/98; webapp bundles cleanly.

title: Context-rich feedback: resolve feedback events to page+element snapshot
description: When user gives feedback during Tier 2 session, attach: current URL, last clicked element + path, DOM snapshot, last 5 actions, last 3 network responses. Agent receives feedback with this enrichment - no follow-up 'where' questions.
close_reason: Session replay store (SP-390): SessionStore.replay() returns chronological event timeline; recentEvents() tails last N for context lookups. New /api/sessions/:id/replay endpoint in review server. Context-rich feedback (SP-d9c): ingestFeedback now optionally fetches recentEvents (configurable count) when sessionId is set and prepends a 'Context (last events leading up to feedback)' block to the observation description so future runs see what the user was looking at. Tests stub the fetcher; default impl opens session-store on the fly. 4 new tests; full suite 84/84.

title: Pattern miner: extract recurring user/agent action sequences
description: Hybrid pipeline: heuristic (frequency-based n-gram of action types across sessions) + LLM-driven (cheap model summarizes 'user repeatedly verified X by doing Y'). Produces candidate-patterns list with examples and frequency stats.
close_reason: Pattern miner shipped in src/agent/pattern-miner.ts. Heuristic n-gram (length 2-4) extractor over the session-store corpus; tracks per-pattern occurrences, distinct sessionCount, capped illustrative examples. Filterable by role / kind-prefix / kind-exclusion. Scoring weights sessionCount over raw count so single-session loops don't dominate. No LLM dependency — synthesizer (SP-a71) decides which candidates earn natural-language descriptions. 5 new tests; full suite 89/89.

title: Session replay store: chronological event log per session
description: Append-only event log per session capturing user actions + agent actions + feedback events on a single timeline. Backed by SQLite. Powers (a) Tier 2 context resolution at feedback time, (b) pattern miner input for skill auto-creation.
close_reason: Session replay store (SP-390): SessionStore.replay() returns chronological event timeline; recentEvents() tails last N for context lookups. New /api/sessions/:id/replay endpoint in review server. Context-rich feedback (SP-d9c): ingestFeedback now optionally fetches recentEvents (configurable count) when sessionId is set and prepends a 'Context (last events leading up to feedback)' block to the observation description so future runs see what the user was looking at. Tests stub the fetcher; default impl opens session-store on the fly. 4 new tests; full suite 84/84.

title: Playwright CDP-attach: shared browser context with user
description: Specify launches Playwright in CDP-attach mode connecting to user Chrome (--remote-debugging-port) OR launches its own headed Chrome the user drives. Captures all user activity: URL transitions, clicks (with DOM snapshot at click time), form values, hover, scroll, network responses.
close_reason: Playwright CDP-attach shipped in src/agent/cdp-attach.ts. attachToUserChrome connects via chromium.connectOverCDP to a user-driven Chrome on  or 9222, returns first context+page, instruments page-level events (browser:navigation, browser:click, browser:input, browser:console) onto the event bus tagged with sessionId. Page observer auto-reinstalls on framenavigated. Detach helper removes instrumentation without closing the user's browser; disconnect closes the Playwright connection only. 4 new tests; full suite 80/80.

title: Confidence model: per-check acceptance rate -> autonomy preset
description: Each verification check accumulates accept/override stats. UI offers presets: ask everything / ask uncertain / autonomous + report. Threshold defaults shift over time. Stored alongside per-spec observations or in user memory file.
close_reason: Pattern propagator (SP-9ei) listens for feedback:propagate_pattern events and injects a sibling-check directive into the active MessageInjector during a verify run, so user-flagged important patterns get applied across siblings without leaving the session. Wired in sdk-runner alongside other learning components. Confidence store (SP-3yl) tallies accept/override per behavior from feedback:ingested events; autonomyDecision combines the rate with a preset (ask_everything / ask_uncertain / autonomous) to choose between asking and silent. 11 new tests; full suite 76/76.

title: In-session sibling-check propagation on important_pattern
description: When user flags 'important_pattern' on element X (e.g. empty state on form 1), agent immediately probes the same pattern across siblings in current session (forms 2..N). Implementation: pattern recognizer extracts (element_kind, check_kind) tuple from feedback, queues new behavior runs against matching elements.
close_reason: Pattern propagator (SP-9ei) listens for feedback:propagate_pattern events and injects a sibling-check directive into the active MessageInjector during a verify run, so user-flagged important patterns get applied across siblings without leaving the session. Wired in sdk-runner alongside other learning components. Confidence store (SP-3yl) tallies accept/override per behavior from feedback:ingested events; autonomyDecision combines the rate with a preset (ask_everything / ask_uncertain / autonomous) to choose between asking and silent. 11 new tests; full suite 76/76.

title: Optional Honcho MCP integration for dialectic user modeling
description: Honcho (plastic-labs, third-party, MIT) provides dialectic user modeling - 'user cares about empty states', 'user always checks accessibility'. Wire as optional MemoryProvider via Honcho's MCP server. Gated behind config flag; default off.
close_reason: Honcho dialectic provider shipped in src/agent/honcho-provider.ts. HonchoMemoryProvider implements MemoryProvider; reads delegate to FileBackedMemoryProvider for structured rows; prefetch additionally calls /v1/apps/<app>/users/<user>/dialectic for the dialectic representation and prepends it to the local prompt; writes still hit the file backend AND best-effort POST observations as events. Configured via HONCHO_URL / HONCHO_APP / HONCHO_USER / HONCHO_TOKEN env vars; sdk-runner picks Honcho automatically when HONCHO_URL is set, otherwise default file-backed. Failures are silent and never break the run. 5 new tests; full suite 108/108.

title: Prompt evolution loop using DSPy/GEPA
description: Periodic background job ingests session traces + accumulated feedback corpus, regenerates skill prompts via DSPy optimizers (GEPA or similar). Versioned skill files committed in repo so regressions are git-trivial to revert. Runs on cron or manual trigger; uses cheap inference (not Max-subscription).
close_reason: Prompt evolution shipped in src/agent/prompt-evolution.ts. heuristicEvolve folds high-confidence observations + overridden-behavior hints into the system prompt; pure-text, no API dep. externalEvolve shells to optional scripts/evolve-prompt.py with JSON corpus on stdin (DSPy/GEPA integration point). evolvePrompt writes versioned prompts to .specify/prompts/<timestamp>_<id>.md so reverts are git-trivial. buildCorpus aggregates observations + confidence + recent sessions. 5 new tests; full suite 103/103. Note: external Python optimiser is opt-in; specify install never depends on Python.

title: Self-evolution cron: scope, frequency, rollback path
description: hermes-agent-self-evolution (DSPy + GEPA) regenerates spec-driven + qa-feedback skill prompts from accumulated session traces + feedback. Schedule via 'hermes cron'. Decisions: how often (weekly?), input corpus size, prompt diff review gate, version + rollback strategy. Versioned skill files in repo so reverts are git-trivial.
